summary: SK-13 (sort products by name and price, Jira Mode B) — E2E-only slice against Saucedemo (sorting is client-side; no backend sort API). Five tests covering the four sort modes (Name A->Z, Name Z->A, Price low->high, Price high->low) plus the active-selection-visible check all passed live: 0 failures, 0 bug drafts. The price-sort assertions compare prices as NUMBERS to guard against string-based ordering (RISK-002). Sort behaviour and the active-option locator were verified live via the playwright-test MCP before authoring.
release_recommendation_reasoning: All three risks are covered_passing. RISK-001 (wrong sort order) is covered by TC-001..TC-004, all passing — each sort mode produces the correct rendered order. RISK-002 (price sorted as strings, e.g. $10 before $9) is specifically guarded by TC-003/TC-004, which parse prices numerically and passed (verified live: lohi -> 7.99..49.99). RISK-003 (active selection not shown) is covered by TC-005 (the control reflects the chosen option). No blocking failures, no flakes, no skipped or unexecuted cases. E2E-only story (Saucedemo sorts client-side), so the flat execution_summary form is used.
